note: Apply to DATA inside js/engine.js. compute() unchanged. DATA.version stays v0.322 (display data only).

drawbackFx_replace_strings.Missing Arm: Lost an arm; defined mechanical penalty. DEX cap: your Dexterity score can never exceed 12. You may only take this drawback if your Dexterity is currently 12 or below.
drawbackFx_replace_strings.Peg Leg: Lost a leg that has been replaced with a peg leg or similar. Your speed reduced by 5ft, athletics and acrobatics checks are made with disadvantage. DEX cap: your Dexterity score can never exceed 12. You may only take this drawback if your Dexterity is currently 12 or below.
drawbackFx_replace_strings.Slow-Footed: Your speed is reduced by 5 feet. Speed only — no effect on Dexterity.
drawbackFx_replace_strings.Berserk Temper: When you first take damage in a fight you fly into a rage, attacking the nearest creature each turn until you succeed on a Wisdom save (DC 12 + your proficiency bonus) at the end of your turn. WIS cap: your Wisdom score can never exceed 10. You may only take this drawback if your Wisdom is currently 10 or below.
drawbackFx_replace_strings.Faltering Resolve: The first saving throw you make in each combat is rolled with disadvantage. WIS cap: your Wisdom score can never exceed 10. You may only take this drawback if your Wisdom is currently 10 or below.
drawbackFx_replace_strings.Reckless: You cannot take the Dodge or the Disengage action. WIS cap: your Wisdom score can never exceed 10. You may only take this drawback if your Wisdom is currently 10 or below.
drawbackFx_replace_strings.Hexed Luck: You can never score a critical hit; your natural 20s count as ordinary hits and don’t earn Heroic Inspiration.
drawbackFx_replace_strings.Mana-Sick: After casting your highest-level spell, disadvantage on your attack roll.
drawbackFx_replace_strings.Soul Debt: A fiend skims the interest on your soul. Whenever you regain Hit Points from a spell, a magic item, or a spent Hit Die, you regain 1d4 fewer (minimum 1) — the rest feeds the debt. The Hit Points you recover at the end of a Long Rest are unaffected.
drawbackFx_replace_strings.Prophesied Doom: Against your foretold doom (a creature type, element, or circumstance — the DM chooses the doom at creation) you have disadvantage on saves and attack rolls. They have advantage on attack rolls.
